Name: VES Solar Enerji
Description:
2023 yılında İzmir’de kurulan VES Solar, 70’li yıllardan günümüze uzanan bir sanayi deneyimine sahip kadronun, makine imalat sanayi sektöründe sergiledikleri uluslararası ölçekteki başarı hikayesini tekrar etmeyi hedefledikleri Güneş Enerjisi Paneli üretimine yönelik yeni girişimidir.

VES Solar, karbon-nötr dönüşüme hayat verecek fotovoltaik güneş panellerini üretmek üzere Pancar Organize Sanayi Bölgesi Torbalı’da kurulu 8.000 metrekarelik tesisinde, alanında en modern teknolojiye sahip 1.Faz yatırımıyla yıllık 300 MW’lik üretim kapasitesine sahip olacaktır.

Fotovoltaik panellerle üretilen elektrik enerjisi, temiz, yaygın, ucuz, kolay ulaşılabilir, tüketildiği noktada üretilebilir, sürdürülebilir, çevre dostu bir enerji kaynağıdır. Merkezi olmayan dağıtık yapısıyla enerji üretimini demokratikleştiren, hane halkını üretici yapan bir teknolojidir. Tüm bu özellikleriyle, diğer yenilenebilir enerji kaynaklarının ötesinde, insanlığın bugünü ve yarınında çok önemli bir yer teşkil etmektedir.

Güneş enerjisinden elektrik üreten panellerde kullanılan teknoloji sürekli gelişmekte, birim alandan elde edilen gücü artıracak şekilde daha verimli teknikler ve materyaller kullanıma sunulmaktadır. Yakın zamanda elde edilmiş bu hızlı gelişime rağmen, teknoloji hala büyük ilerlemelere gebedir. Biz de gelişime açık bu dinamik sektörde kendi özgün katkımızı sunmayı arzuluyoruz.

Name: Tillquist Group AB
Description:
Tillquist Group AB is a quality focused distributor of technology components and systems, mainly in the energy storage, power, industrial automation and photonics industries. Today we primarily represent selected European and American suppliers, with customers predominantly in Scandinavia and EMEA. We offer both customized and standardized products and services. All our partners and customers are among the top brands in their respective industries. We also develop and produce our wholly-owned product portfolio within transducer for the global energy sector. To increase sales and local know-how, we have sales distributors in selected geographical areas.  

Tillquist Group AB was founded in Old Town, Stockholm, 1895. Today we reside in Kista, a tech hub north of Stockholm with two regional offices, Nyköping and Gothenburg. We have also a sales agent in Helsinki, Finland. 

Business segments: Energy metering, Energy storage (Battery Energy Storage Systems/ BESS), Motion Control (formerly Nanor AB), Photonics (formerly Optonyx AB), Power automation, Process automation, Test & Measurement (Instrumentcenter Sweden AB). 

For more information, visit: www.tillquist.com

Name: Grace BESS
Description:

Name: HEVATECH
Description:
HEVATECH est une start-up technologique focalisée sur la valorisation des rejets thermiques industriels.
Elle a développé deux technologies innovantes de valorisation de chaleur fatale industrielle en électricité et cogénération (chaud / froid) : TURBOSOL et H2P. Il s’agit de 2 procédés brevetés propriétaires basés sur des cycles thermodynamiques utilisant des fluides non polluants et sans risques, de l’air, de l’eau et de l’huile végétale. La chaleur est captée par des échangeurs standards et ensuite convertie en électricité via une turbine à basse vitesse dans un cas, et un moteur à piston dans l’autre, tous 2 tournant à 1500 tr/mn pour produire de l’électricité à 50 Hz. La particularité de ces technologies est de produire en même temps que l’électricité de la chaleur valorisable sous différentes formes pour le process industriel, pour du chauffage bâtiment, ou dans un réseau de chaleur urbaine.
Nous proposons actuellement des solutions de valorisation de chaleur fatale de 300 à 1000°C et dans une gamme de puissance de 10 à 300 KWe. Une extension est prévue jusqu’à 1MWe. Toutes deux permettent de réaliser d'importantes économies d'énergie (électricité et gaz), avec un fort impact de décarbonation. Nos machines se financent avec un payback très attractif du fait du fort taux de valorisation de chaleur fatale (jusque 70% en cogénération). Nous ciblons prioritairement les industries de transformation (Sidérurgie, verrerie, fonderie, céramiques, matériaux spéciaux, …), incinération de déchets, recyclage batteries,  et groupes électrogènes ou marins.

Ratings reach up to IP67 for dust and water protection, and liquid-cooled designs can deliver up to 700 A where sites require higher currents. All products follow international standards and carry certifications such as CE, TÜV, UL, UKCA, CB, and CQC.

Why partners choose us

• 18+ years of manufacturing experience
• Automated, lean production for consistent quality and competitive cost

Global reach

Workersbee serves customers in more than 30 countries through a growing network of distributors and partners in North America, Europe, and Asia.

Our commitment

Name: HutanBio
Description:
HutanBio is developing the world’s first cost-parity, net-negative sustainable fuel for aviation, shipping, and heavy transport. These sectors underpin $40 trillion in global trade and represent a ~$960 billion fuel market by 2035. Yet no sustainable aviation fuel (SAF), maritime fuel, or heavy-duty transport fuel has proven to be cheap, scalable and sustainable at global scale.

Until now.

HutanBio’s breakthrough platform, HBx, is built on a new-to-science marine microalgae biofuel system evolved for extreme environments. Unlike legacy algae systems that failed to scale, HBx delivers ~25× higher oil productivity, exceptional CO₂ capture and carbon removal, and stable operation in high heat, high salinity - eliminating dependence on arable land or freshwater.

Why HutanBio can scale where others failed.

Biological step-change: HBx strains originate from a marine extremophile with 300 photosynthetic layers, enabling sustained productivity under peak desert sunlight while concentrating oil in protected cellular structures for cleaner fuel extraction

Cost-engineered production: Patented, vertical, enclosed photobioreactors are purpose-designed for long-life desert deployment, automation, and low OPEX at industrial scale

Proven in practice: >200 tonnes of algae cultivated, 18+ months of uninterrupted operation, real-time yield optimisation, and zero system crashes

Ideal composition for transport fuel: HBx Bio Oil (C16–C18 TAGs) enables up to 90% blends for marine & heavy-duty diesel, requiring only limited refining to meet aviation fuel standards

Modular by design, scaling from 1-ha units to multi-km² bio-energy sites across coastal desert regions between 40°N and 40°S. Just 7% of suitable coastal desert land could supply all global aviation and maritime fuel demand.

 Welcoming engagement from Series A investors, strategic partners, and industrial collaborators aligned with scaling next-generation biofuels globally.

Welcome to Redeem Solar Technologies – Pioneering the Future of Chemistry!

 Founded in February 2023 and proudly based in Graz, Austria, Redeem Solar Technologies is at the forefront of transforming the chemical industry through cutting-edge innovations in flow chemistry and catalysis. Our mission is to unleash the power of innovation in chemical transformations, making significant strides towards a sustainable and eco-friendly future.

 We specialize in developing groundbreaking, patent-protected modular and stand-alone photocatalytic reactors. These reactors harness the abundant energy of the sun to drive chemical processes, with a primary focus on producing hydrogen, fertilizers, and a range of valuable chemicals. Our revolutionary units are already shaping the landscape of chemistry at prestigious institutions such as the University of Graz, the Technical University Vienna, and North Carolina State University.

 At Redeem Solar Technologies, we are proud to be supported by key players in the innovation ecosystem. We extend our gratitude to aws (Austria Wirtschaftsservice) and aws incubator, the Austrian Research Promotion Agency (FFG), the European Space Agency Business Incubation Centre (esa BIC), the Styrian Business Promotion Agency (SFG), and the Federal Ministry of the Republic of Austria for Climate Action, Environment, Energy, Mobility, Innovation, and Technology.

Join us on our journey as we drive sustainable progress, redefine the boundaries of photochemistry, and contribute to a cleaner, greener future for all.

At PowerOptimal, we’re building the future of energy—one innovation at a time. Founded in 2014, we specialise in smart, sustainable solutions built on South African engineering and IP, with local manufacturing at our core.

Name: Tobe Energy
Description:
At Tobe Energy, we’ve reinvented electrolysis from the ground up to unlock a new era of clean, cost-effective hydrogen. Our proprietary technology delivers dramatically higher efficiency than traditional systems, making zero-emission hydrogen viable for industrial use, 24/7 power, and the energy demands of tomorrow’s data-driven world.

Founded by a team of engineers and scientists with deep experience in oil & gas, renewables, and deep tech, we’ve spent years developing a high-performance resonant electrolysis system that operates like a finely tuned LLC converter—minimizing energy losses, increasing throughput, and scaling with precision.

We don’t just build electrolyzers. We build hydrogen infrastructure—turnkey units capable of co-locating with data centers, industrial sites, and energy hubs to supply low-cost, clean hydrogen without the need for long-distance transport or storage.

Tobe is now scaling production with a 10,000 sq ft facility in Oklahoma, aiming to deliver our first 100 commercial units. Our hydrogen is already priced 5x below market rates—and we’re just getting started.

We believe the future of energy should be renewable, reliable, and financially rational. Hydrogen can get us there—if we build it right.

⸻

 Redefining Electrolysis. Delivering Scalable Green Hydrogen.

Description:
Armour Edge is a game-changing solution to the costly problem of leading edge erosion on wind turbine blades: precision engineered shields are securely bonded to the leading edge of blades to provide an aerodynamically optimal, full-life protection that minimises maintenance costs and maximises energy production and return on investment.

Armour Edge is easy to install, maintains the blades’ aerodynamic properties and is extremely durable, with an estimated lifespan of over 20 years.

The shields are made from a bespoke version of INEOS Styrolution’s ultra-tough Luran SC thermoplastic material, created specifically for wind turbine blades.

Each Armour Edge shield is up to one meter long and is custom formed to the exact blade type and then bonded into place by trained technicians. This allows rapid installation which results in less downtime and improved profitability for wind farm owners.

Description:
Wer wir sind:

In Zeiten von extrem hohen Energiekosten und der damit notwendigen Energiewende steigt auch der Bedarf an eine angepasste, individuelle und wirtschaftlich optimal ausgelegte Gebäudetechnik. 

Unser Planungsbüro besteht seit 1992 und gilt als kompetenter, zuverlässiger und flexibler Partner für das gesamte Spektrum der technischen Gebäudeausrüstung (KG 410-480).

Mit unseren hochqualifizierten Mitarbeitern bieten wir ökologisch, wirtschaftliche Planungsdienstleistungen für Gebäudetypen aller Art an.

Aus langjähriger Erfahrung haben wir die Kompetenz, Projekte in allen Größenordnungen zu begleiten und alle Leistungsphasen der Kostengruppe 400 darzustellen.

Was wir tun:

Beratung, Betreuung und Unterstützung von Bauherrn, Architekten, Unternehmen, öffentliche Auftraggeber bei der Anpassung an den schnellen, technologischen und ökologischen Wandel.

Verschiedenste Planungsdienstleistungen bezüglich Umsetzung und Einbindung erneuerbarer Energiekonzepte in der integralen Gebäudetechnik.

Qualität, Wirtschaftlichkeit, eine hohe Kundenzufriedenheit, sowie der gemeinschaftliche Projekterfolg haben für uns die höchste Priorität.

Name: VH Quatrevingtreize
Description:
Created in 2017, VH Quatrevingtreize initially developed and patented hydroturbines, made of biosourced and recyclable materials, transforming water movements into renewable energy. Our technology was validated in real conditions in Provence’ rivers.

In a context of climate change acceleration, observed in rivers in the past 2-3 years with a growing variation of dry episodes or flooding, we adapted our strategy to move away from static energy production ; in 2023 we designed an autonomous sailing vessel, equipped with our hydroturbines -that are located under the hull-, to transform wave movements into renewable energy. 

Thanks to its mobility, to AI, to weather forecast, the vessel is able to harvest energy where and when it is present, optimizing its production while being able to protect itself from extreme events. 

Once produced, the energy can be stored in batteries onboard and provided offshore to ocean-users: industry, research and defense. The produced energy can also be shipped to isolated sites and state islands.

Name: W'wave
Description:
W'wave optimizes wind in wind farms.

We provide patented technology to optimize existing wind conditions in wind farms, so that wind operators and developers can:
·     maximize its energy output,
·     minimize the cost of energy,
·     build more powerful wind farms (installing more turbines) in a given development area,
·     build more compact wind farms (installing the same number of turbines and power in a reduced development area), avoiding conflictive areas.

Our concept materializes in WindWaves: a new hardware component for wind farms, supported by in-house computational fluid and product software. We have different product models:

Permanent WindWaves: made of steel and anchored to the ground, they are designed to last >20 years. Different models adapt to the different site conditions. Potential hybridization with solar PV modules to increase efficiency of wind farm even further. 
Temporary WindWaves (a.k.a. BioWindWaves): made of agricultural straw bales, they are meant to be installed temporarily in a wind farm and replaced by new straw bales periodically. BioWindWaves has relevant synergies and simbiosis with the agricultural, livestock and biomass/biogas industries, by representing a way to stock economically agricultural straw in the long-term, to be reused in its traditional applications when straw is most needed (i.e.: when short-term stocks are depleted e.g.: due to ever more frequent droughts due to Climate Change).

With our innovation we are in a mission to contribute to a better, more competitive wind industry to continue the World's effort to fight #climatechange.
 
We are scaling up our operations and are open to #partnerships with wind operators, wind developers, wind turbine & component manufacturers, wind farm construction players and #cleantech investors.

Bei den Entwicklungen setzt man unter anderem auf die wertvolle, regional verfügbare Ressource Biomasse und leistet damit einen wichtigen Beitrag zum Klimaschutz und zur Netzsicherheit. Die zuverlässigen Strom-Wärmekraftwerke und hochwertigen Batteriespeicher kommen vor allem in den unterschiedlichen Bereichen von Industrie, Gewerbe, Tourismus, Landwirtschaft und in Energiegemeinschaften zum Einsatz.

Name: Spanner Re² GmbH
Description:
Spanner Re² GmbH ist der Marktführer im Bereich Strom- und Wärmeerzeugung aus Biomasse und Reststoffen. Weltweit sind mehr als 1.000 Anlagen installiert, die nach dem Prinzip der Kraft-Wärme-Kopplung dezentral Strom und Wärme erzeugen, wenn diese auch benötigt wird. 

Mit der Re² Technik lässt sich auch ein Synthesegas (Syngas) erzeugen, das anstelle von Erdgas für industrielle Thermoprozesse eingesetzt werden kann und der Industrie eine nachhaltige und CO2-neutrale Energiegewinnungsmöglichkeit bietet.

Produziert werden die Anlagen sowie die gesamte Peripherie wie z.B. Austragungs- und Fördertechnik sowie vollautomatische Brennstofftrockner am Firmenstandort im niederbayerischen Neufahrn. Das Unternehmen versteht sich als Komplettanbieter und hat für nahezu jeden Anwendungsbereich Energielösungen von 35 kWel bis zu mehreren Megawatt elektrisch im Portfolio. Die bewährten KWK-Anlagen sind der ideale Partner für Solar- oder Windenergie und liefern auch nachts oder bei Windstille Energie - und das aus unterschiedlichsten Brennstoffen wie aus Waldrestholz, Straßenbegleitgrün, Paletten, Obstkisten, Altholz, Kernen oder auch aus Kunststoffabfällen u.v.m. Dies bietet vielen Unternehmen die Möglichkeit, ihre Wertschöpfungskette zu optimieren und ihre Reststoffe gewinnbringend zu verwerten. Mit den Energieanlagen von Re² werden die Betriebskosten für Strom und Wärme langfristig planbar und die Energieversorgung kann autark erfolgen. 

Mit innovativer Technik, gelebter Nachhaltigkeit und kontinuierlicher Motivation Energiegewinnung stets neu zu Denken - getreu ihrer Mission "Rethink energy" - steht das Unternehmen für ressourcenschonende Energielösungen am Puls der Zeit. 

Als weiteres Standbein entwickelt und vertreibt Re² moderne Pelletheizungen mit einer Nennwärmeleistung von 4 bis 100 kW thermisch und blickt dabei auf über 20 Jahre Erfahrung in der Entwicklung und Fertigung zurück.

Name: PWRstation Holding
Description:
Since its inception in Switzerland in 2014, PWRstation has been dedicated to designing and developing innovative solutions for solar energy deployment. We stand out with our unique approach to "responsive solar" technology, enabling solar panels to adapt in real-time to their environment to maximize energy production while minimizing environmental and economic impacts.

Our flagship products, Dyptic and Tryptic, are prefabricated, modular solar solutions designed to meet the diverse needs of today’s market. Dyptic is tailored for semi-permanent projects, particularly for industrial land awaiting development or for flat roofs. Engineered for quick deployment, ballast mounting, and easy disassembly, Dyptic addresses the challenges of temporary installations by enabling maximum energy production without long-term commitments.

Tryptic, on the other hand, incorporates patented solar tracking and self-protection technology. Particularly suited for agrivoltaic applications, Tryptic allows solar panels to adjust based on the needs of the underlying crops or weather conditions. The panels can fold up in the event of hail or strong winds, ensuring optimal protection and extended longevity. This flexibility opens up new synergies between solar energy production and agriculture, especially in densely populated areas where available land is limited.

We believe in a collaborative business model. By offering licensing opportunities and partnering with local entities, PWRstation aims to create solar solutions that benefit both businesses and local communities, while reducing transportation costs and fostering job creation.

Today, our vision is to focus on development within Switzerland before expanding our model internationally, with the mission to support the world towards a sustainable and efficient energy transition.

Join us on this journey and discover how PWRstation is redefining solar energy standards for a greener and smarter future.

Name: Sahanease Solar
Description:
SahanEase Solar, a subsidiary of Sahan Investment Group (formerly SahanTech Solutions), is a leading manufacturer of solar energy solutions in Ethiopia. As Ethiopia’s first licensed off-grid solar energy manufacturing company under the Ethiopian Commercial Registration and Business License Proclamation No. 980/2016, SahanEase Solar is committed to expanding clean energy access across the country.

Founded by experts and former leaders of the African Continental Power Project, a think tank dedicated to electrification in Africa, SahanEase Solar focuses on local manufacturing, sustainable innovation, and rural empowerment through affordable, high-quality solar technologies.

Vision

To become Africa’s leading provider of locally manufactured off-grid solar energy solutions and sustainable agricultural technologies, empowering rural communities with clean energy and innovative farming solutions.

Mission

Manufacture and distribute affordable, high-quality solar energy solutions for underserved communities.

Develop SahanEase Solar Generators and PV Panels, providing reliable, locally made power solutions.

Pioneer SahanEco Hydroponics, enabling water-efficient agriculture and increasing food security.

Drive economic growth by fostering local innovation, job creation, and skills development in the renewable energy sector.

Contribute to climate resilience by promoting renewable energy adoption and reducing dependence on fossil fuels.


Our Products & Solutions

 SahanEase Solar Generator – Portable, efficient, and cost-effective energy for rural households and businesses.
 SahanEase PV Panels – High-performance, locally manufactured solar panels tailored for Ethiopia’s energy needs.
 SahanEco Hydroponics – A revolutionary water-efficient farming system for sustainable agriculture.

With a strong commitment to energy access, sustainability, and innovation, SahanEase Solar is transforming lives and shaping Ethiopia’s renewable energy future.

Name: Spunvertek
Description:
Spunvertek Limited is a renewable energy and agri-tech company driving sustainable farming in Nigeria and beyond. We design and deploy solar-powered irrigation systems that help farmers boost yields, cut costs, and farm year-round without relying on fossil fuels.

Through our innovative lease-to-own model, farmers access solar water pumping systems with minimal upfront cost, paying over five years — often with part of their harvest — before gaining full ownership.

We manufacture smart controllers for efficient irrigation management, work closely with rural communities, and focus on improving livelihoods while promoting climate-friendly agriculture.

 Our Mission: Empower farmers with clean energy solutions that drive productivity, resilience, and food security.
 Our Vision: A future where every farm has access to affordable, sustainable irrigation.

Name: Batcapt
Description:
Batcapt offers monitoring products and solutions for high-voltage batteries, designed for professionals responsible for managing these systems. We provide diagnostic, safety, and degradation prevention services, particularly when batteries are disassembled.
Electrification is accelerating across industries. These batteries are now predominantly found in electric vehicles and stationary energy storage systems used to stabilize electrical grids. Battery usage grows annually, creating substantial European volumes of batteries in transit, storage, and awaiting recycling. We deliver solutions to simplify and secure these critical operations.
High-voltage batteries present unique logistical challenges that demand specialized tools.

Name: Heatlift

Born from a shared ambition and a strong technical legacy, Heatlift was founded by 10 former core engineers from Enertime.
United by a common goal — to maximize their collective potential and drive meaningful innovation — they created Heatlift to design next-generation thermal machines that meet the highest standards of performance, reliability, and environmental impact.

Name: ReGaWatt GmbH
Description: ReGaWatt GmbH plans and builds highly-efficient energy systems based on all types of biomass made from mechanical pulp. The technological core component is an updraft gasifier with a fuel performance of 1 to 5 MW. The combination of this highly robust gasifier with different modules deigned to ensure clean combustion, gas cleaning systems, gas engines, ORC system, heat recovery, etc. gave rise to the Kombi Power System. References can be found in Germany, Switzerland, the Netherlands and Japan. After a 10-year development process, pilot systems and second generation commercial systems, our aim is too make the Kombi Power System more cost effective and fuel-flexible. To this end, a small test system – fully equipped with gas cooling and ESP – has been designed and built. Thanks to this test system, we can now test and assess special customer fuels.

Name: Konvekta - Energierückgewinnung
Description:
Konvekta ist Technologieführer in der Energierückgewinnung mit Kreislaufverbundsystemen, die aussergewöhnlich hohe Jahresnutzungsgrade von oft 90+% erzielen. Basis für die hohe Effizienz sind ausgereifte Technologien in der Software (Auslegung/Optimierung/Regelung), wie auch der Hardware (insbesondere Wärmeübertrager). Alle Komponenten werden von Konvekta im Schweizer Mutterhaus produziert/entwickelt - dadurch können die dynamischen Berechnungen auf die exakten Kennlinienfelder zurückgreifen und erzielen so die nötige Präzision.
Die Systeme werden anwendungsspezifisch hergestellt und für alle 8760 Stunden des Jahres eines Standortes optimiert. Mit der umfassenden Simulation können bereits frühzeitig Netto-Energie-/CO2-Einsparungen und ROI ausgewiesen werden für Planung und Fördergelder. Mit dem Zero Emission Energy Recovery System können Gebäude sogar ohne fossile Brennstoffe gekühlt und beheizt werden. Mit Ihren Systemen trägt Konvekta signifikant zur Energiewende bei.

Name: Canopus Drilling Solutions
Description:
Canopus, founded in 2019, developed a game-changing cost-effective drilling technology which improves the productivity of geothermal reservoirs by a factor 2 to 6. The Canopus drilling technology is based on fast and accurate directional steel shot drilling. This enables drilling through any rock type and does not require weight or torque on the drill bit. The proprietary method for steering the rotating drill bit is accurate, consumes minimal power and the autonomous steering device is a 3m long assembly, right behind the drill bit.

This unique drilling capability enables drilling of long horizontal multilaterals in geothermal reservoirs, thereby improving the reservoir contact at low cost. The drilling technology is effective in all rock types. The directional capability of the Canopus steel shot drilling technology can be used for constructing shallow curved closed loop heat exchangers, deep open geothermal systems, and closed loop ultradeep geothermal wells for power and heat production. Canopus plans to open up many geothermal opportunities that previously were not economically feasible.

Name: Tansun Limited
Description:
Tansun has been at the forefront of infrared heating for over 40 years, with a rich heritage of British excellence. Our partnership with Phillips in 1990 pioneered the world's first infrared IP waterproof heater, and we have since continued to push boundaries. 

Our invention of the first waterproof IR lamp with General Electric revolutionised the way for new applications of infrared heating in 1997. Today, electrical contractors request Tansun products for their reliability and we remain the only company manufacturing high-efficiency shortwave heaters in the UK. With our heritage of innovation and dedication to quality, Tansun is the clear choice for those seeking the very best in infrared heating to reduce carbon emissions. We are committed to investing in the R&D of superior energy efficient systems which help industries meet net zero targets.

Our bespoke design service allows us to meet the requirements of each distinct installation. Our highly experienced and knowledgeable in-house specialists can conduct a site survey accounting for space characteristics such as size, layout and usage patterns; producing the most effective heating application possible. We also provide bespoke designs including frames, brackets, controllability, and presence detectors, ensuring a perfect fit for your heating system and greater energy efficiency.

With over 40 years’ experience, Tansun is an established "END-TO-END" solutions provider, manufacturing high-efficiency heaters centrally from the West Midlands, designing complete heating solutions, and working with trusted installers to turn around a net zero project seamlessly. Our range of infrared heating products is comprehensive, with one of the largest selections of commercial, warehouse, and industrial heaters available all made in the UK. Today, Tansun serves markets globally, earning a reputation as the longest UK established go-to leader.

See https://www.tansun.com/gb_en/ for more information and get in touch.

Name: PEACE H2 project
Description:
Pressurized Efficient Alkaline Electrolyser (PEACE) is a research and innovation project funded under the EU Horizon Europe programme by the Clean Hydrogen Partnership. The PEACE project is being carried out by seven partners under the coordination of the Deutsches Zentrum für Luft- und Raumfahrt (DLR). 
The PEACE project focuses on developing high-pressure alkaline electrolysis technology to substantially reduce hydrogen production costs, aiming to achieve costs below 3 €/kg to enhance the competitiveness of the hydrogen economy. Furthermore, the project aims to enhance the integration of the hydrogen production with its subsequent utilisation within the chemical industry. Such an integration reduces costs and save energy by at least 8%. PEACE will also conduct a life cycle assessment (LCA) to quantify the environmental impacts of its innovative technology.
The PEACE project is supported by the Clean Hydrogen Partnership and its members. Co-funded by the European Union.
Views and opinions expressed are those of the project consortium and do not necessarily reflect those of the European Union or Clean Hydrogen Partnership. Neither the European Union nor the granting authority can be held responsible for them.

Name: PEPPERONI_EU
Description:
PEPPERONI is a four-year Research and Innovation project co-funded by the European Union under Horizon Europe and supported by the Swiss State Secretariat for Education, Research and Innovation that started on 1 November 2022. PEPPERONI will advance the perovskite/silicon tandem photovoltaics (PV) technology towards market introduction and mass manufacturing. The project, coordinated by Helmholtz-Zentrum Berlin (DE) and Qcells (DE) will identify and address the barriers to tandem solar technology’s market introduction, and ultimately lay the foundations for fast implementation of new production capacity in Europe as a cost-effective and resource-efficient solution to decarbonise the energy system. The PEPPERONI consortium counts 17 partners from 12 European countries and it combines knowledge and expertise from fundamental research to small-scale testing and development of solar cells all the way to high-throughput industrial manufacturing of large solar modules. 

Funded by the European Union and supported by the Swiss State Secretariat for Education, Research and Innovation (SERI).
